data.body:
Plan-review advisory dispositions (round 4 PASS, 20 advisories; claim gate: plan-review attestation certified):

ACCEPTED + applied to plan before claim:
- E4 (job names): corrected test-wheel/test-sdist -> wheel_test/sdist_test throughout.
- G1G2 (scope): added tests/unit/test_release_workflow_pins.py to Scope; noted existing 08a8/6168 invariants stay green (authorize job is contents:read-only, no new zizmor suppression; publish hash-gate + mcp split untouched bar the if: re-key).
- G6 (tag): specified github_release drops --verify-tag and creates the lightweight v* tag at the run SHA post-publish.
- T8 (preflight schema): documented exact field paths (protection_rules[].type==required_reviewers + non-empty reviewers; deployment_branch_policy.custom_branch_policies==true; branch_policies==exactly {main}).
- T8 (reviewer body): documented PUT reviewers shape [{type:User,id:<numeric>}] + gh api users/<login> --jq .id resolution.
- F1 (Gerrit-lands AC): re-tagged [operator-attested] with change-URL+vote-timestamps evidence (autonomously recordable by the agent driving Gerrit).

ACCEPTED as implementation notes (already handled in code, no further plan text needed):
- T8/T5b (preflight on non-2xx/transient API errors): fail-closed is correct — gh api exits nonzero on HTTP error, aborting the authorize step; a release should NOT proceed when env protection can't be read.
- T3 (guard-fire on non-main ref): verification will push a throwaway branch carrying this release.yml to the mirror so gh workflow run --ref <branch> exercises the ref guard; the mismatched-version case runs on main.

REJECTED / no-op (advisory over-specification):
- T10/T5c/T4/T6 (autonomous env config / approval interaction / destructive trigger removal): the trigger removal + env re-key are the story's deliberate, reviewed design (Finding 1); rollback is documented (re-add push:tags is a one-line revert). No plan change.
- E1/E2/P6/T8-'four'/G1G2-dup/#16-20 (wording/near-duplicate ACs): cosmetic; the ACs are individually observable with proving commands. Left as-is to avoid churn.
